cases.title: TS-20: Mobile web check-in from iOS Safari or Android Chrome client marks Present without native app
preconditions: Active session with valid room GPS (10.762622, 106.660172) and gpsRadiusMeters 100
Enrolled Student authenticated with valid session cookie simulating mobile web client
Valid unexpired QrToken from GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current qrPayload wecheck://check-in?token=uuid&session=uuid
GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight returns 200 outcome Valid for enrolled student
Student coordinates within radius (10.762700, 106.660200)
Request includes spoofMetadata.platform ios or android per mobile client
TestClock within attendance window (scheduledStart + 10 min)
steps: GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight with Student session and assert HTTP 200 outcome Valid with session summary
POST /api/v1/check-in with tokenId from QR payload, in-radius coordinates, and spoofMetadata.platform ios using Student session
Assert HTTP status 200, outcome Success, and attendance.status Present
Issue fresh Valid token for Android client assertion
POST /api/v1/check-in with spoofMetadata.platform android and assert HTTP 200 outcome Success
Verify no native-app-only headers or install tokens required in request or response
Query qr_tokens and assert each tokenId status Consumed
cases.expected: Testing-plan §3 TS-20 satisfied on mobile web clients: preflight pass then functional check-in via QR token and GPS without native app install per NFR-18 target and AC-07a

cases.title: CheckInService.submit accepts mobile web locationPayload with platform metadata and commits Present in PostgreSQL
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database running via Compose
Active session with enrolled student Pending attendance and Valid qr_tokens row
CheckInService wired to AttendanceService, QrTokenRepository, and geo radius validator
locationPayload includes latitude, longitude, and spoofMetadata { mockLocationDetected: false, platform: "ios" } simulating mobile Safari client
steps: Record beforeState: attendance status Pending, qr_token status Valid
Invoke CheckInService.submit(tokenId, locationPayload, studentId) with in-radius mobile coordinates
Query attendance_records for sessionId and studentId and assert status Present with checkedInAt set
Query qr_tokens for tokenId and assert status Consumed
Query check_in_attempts and assert outcome Success with spoof_flags JSONB capturing platform hints per database design §5.10
Assert raw latitude/longitude not stored in attendance_records per NFR-12
cases.expected: NFR-18 module boundary: mobile web GPS payload processed through full check-in pipeline; Present + Consumed atomically per FR-07 and FR-08 without native SDK

cases.title: Mobile check-in pipeline transitions Attendance Pending to Present after GPS radius pass
preconditions: Pending attendance_records row in PostgreSQL for enrolled studentId and Active sessionId
Valid qr_tokens row with status Valid linked to sessionId
Geo validator configured with session room point and 100 m radius
Mobile client coordinates within radius per testing-plan §11 geo fixtures
steps: Record beforeState Attendance Pending and QrToken Valid from database
Invoke CheckInService.submit with tokenId and mobile in-radius locationPayload (platform android)
Query attendance_records and assert afterState Present with non-null checkedInAt
Query qr_tokens and assert afterState Consumed
Verify GPS radius validation executed before Present commit per validation-rules §5.1
cases.expected: beforeState Pending/Valid; afterState Present/Consumed; NFR-18 GPS verification functional at integration layer per FR-08 on mobile web payload

cases.title: POST /api/v1/check-in from mobile web client returns HTTP 200 Success with Present attendance DTO
preconditions: API running on port 3001 against Compose PostgreSQL
Authenticated enrolled Student session cookie with mobile User-Agent (iPhone Safari or Android Chrome)
Active sessionId with Valid QrToken and student Pending attendance
GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight returns 200 outcome Valid
Request body includes tokenId, latitude 10.762700, longitude 106.660200, spoofMetadata.platform ios
steps: GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight with mobile User-Agent and Student session; assert HTTP 200 outcome Valid
Send POST /api/v1/check-in with mobile client payload and Student session cookie
Assert HTTP status 200 OK and Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8
Assert response body outcome Success and message Điểm danh thành công
Assert response body attendance object contains status Present and checkedInAt ISO 8601 UTC string
Assert no errorCode field on success envelope and no native-app redirect URL in response
cases.expected: HTTP 200 mobile web check-in contract per API design §6.2; preflight then submit path consumable by iOS Safari and Android Chrome clients per NFR-18 and BR-15

cases.title: Student mobile Button variants on /login and /check-in use token fg/bg pairs per §3.2.1
preconditions: Authenticated and unauthenticated Student fixtures available
Routes exposing mobile student Button variants: /login (primary, outline), /check-in submit row (primary), error recovery CTAs (primary on outcome panels)
Mobile viewport 320×568 on iOS Safari and Android 10+ Chrome emulation per ui-visual-verification.md
steps: On /login at 320px verify `primary` variant: `--color-primary-600` bg, `--color-primary-foreground` label with ≥4.5:1
On /login verify `outline` variant if present: `--color-primary-600` text/border on `--color-surface-default` with ≥4.5:1
On /check-in gps_capture ready substate verify Xác nhận điểm danh `primary` submit uses authoritative token pair per 05-common-ui-components §2.1
Trigger GpsDisabled or ExpiredQr outcome and verify recovery CTA `primary` variant maintains `--color-primary-foreground` on `--color-primary-600` over semantic wash background
Verify disabled submit during `submitting` substate uses `--color-text-disabled` on `--color-surface-muted` at ≥3:1
Capture 320px screenshots on iOS Safari and Android Chrome; flag any ad-hoc opacity or off-token shades weakening contrast on student mobile routes
cases.expected: NFR-18 mobile web Button contract: student-visible primary, outline, and recovery CTAs on iOS Safari and Android Chrome use documented foreground/background token pairs from 04-design-tokens §3.2.1 — no low-contrast off-token styling per production-ui-quality-bar §4

cases.title: Mobile browser SessionMismatch preflight blocks GPS step for mismatched deep-link session id
preconditions: Authenticated enrolled Student on iOS Safari or Android Chrome mobile viewport 375px width
Active session A with valid QrToken bound to session A
Second session B exists with different sessionId
Deep link /check-in?token=:tokenId&session=:sessionBId uses token from session A with mismatched session query param
steps: Navigate to /check-in?token=:tokenId&session=:sessionBId on mobile viewport
Verify validating_token spinner during GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight?sessionId=:sessionBId
Assert preflight returns HTTP 403 with errorCode SessionMismatch per API design §6.2 and AC-07f
Verify GpsCaptureStep does not mount (no data-testid gps-capture-step or GPS permission prompt) per BR-15
Verify CheckInOutcomePanel SessionMismatch variant with Vietnamese guidance and recovery CTA
Assert qr_tokens row for tokenId remains status Valid (preflight is read-only)
Verify no Present attendance created server-side
cases.expected: NFR-18 mobile web: TS-21c and AC-07f satisfied — session id mismatch rejected at preflight on iOS Safari and Android Chrome; GPS capture unreachable before valid token binding per BR-15
